---
title: "Executive Summary"
---

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
## The One-Paragraph Version

We built **Cedant**, a local-first AI research assistant for reinsurance underwriting. 

Underwriters spend hours digging through regulatory statements and peer reports to find the exact paragraph they need to cite. Cedant changes this: it retrieves the relevant document sections, generates an answer where **every factual claim is backed by a verifiable citation**, and **refuses to guess** when the documents don’t contain the answer. 

Everything runs privately on a single laptop with no data leaving your machine. On a 70-question benchmark it achieved strong citation discipline (zero hallucinated citations on the best model) and perfect refusals, while being transparent about its current limitations — particularly on complex cross-document questions.

Cedant is an initial proof-of-concept that demonstrates a better way to build trustworthy AI tools for regulated domains.
:::

---

## The Problem {#sec-exec-problem}

Reinsurance underwriting research is, in large part, **document work**. An underwriter assessing a risk must repeatedly consult regulatory supervisory statements, peer reinsurers’ sustainability reports, and internal policies — then cite specific paragraphs in committee papers or regulator submissions.

The core problem is **citation discipline**. A free-form summary that “sounds right” but cannot be traced back to an exact paragraph creates risk and extra verification work. A tool that hallucinates citations is actively dangerous under time pressure.

Cedant was designed around three real-world constraints:
- **Data sensitivity** — nothing can leave the underwriter’s laptop.
- **Source verifiability** — every claim must point to a real paragraph.
- **Honest evaluation** — we must measure what the system actually does, including when it should refuse.

---

## What We Built {#sec-exec-what}

Cedant is a focused, single-machine system with two core promises:

1. **Cited answers** — Every factual claim includes a `[chunk_id]` link to the exact document section used.
2. **Honest refusals** — If the documents don’t contain the answer, the system says exactly:  
   *"I cannot answer this from the provided sources."*

It uses hybrid search (semantic + keyword) over a curated set of regulatory and corporate documents, a local language model, and structural validation to enforce both promises.

---

## Key Results {#sec-exec-results}

On the production model (Gemma 4 31B):

- **Citation quality**: 0.598 overall, rising to **0.798** when retrieval succeeds and **1.000** on straightforward single-paragraph questions.
- **Refusals**: **100% correct** (26/26) across deliberately tricky test cases.
- **Hallucinations**: **Zero** fabricated citations on the main model.
- **Speed**: ~23 seconds per answer (a faster alternative is available).

**Main strengths**: Excellent on single-document regulatory and corporate questions with perfect citation trustworthiness.  
**Known limitations**: Retrieval misses on 25% of questions and weaker performance on complex cross-document synthesis (clearly documented).

---

## What This Is Not {#sec-exec-not}

Cedant is an **initial research prototype**, not a finished production tool. It excels at showing what trustworthy cited AI can look like, but should not be used for live underwriting decisions today.

Full limitations and future work are detailed later in the report.

---

## Project at a Glance {#sec-exec-glance}

| | |
|---|---|
| **Domain** | Reinsurance underwriting research |
| **Indexed corpus** | 6 PDFs from 4 issuers (PRA × 3, EIOPA × 1, Munich Re × 1, Swiss Re × 1); 15.3 MB; 461 chunks |
| **Synthetic carve-out** | 3 Sycamore Reinsurance documents drafted, not indexed in v1 |
| **Retrieval** | Hybrid BGE-M3 dense + BM25 sparse, fused via RRF k=60; top_k=5 |
| **Vector store** | Local Qdrant (filesystem-backed) |
| **Generation** | Local oMLX, OpenAI-compatible chat completions; temperature 0.0; production default Gemma 4 31B IT 6-bit MLX |
| **Alternate model** | Qwen3.6 35B-A3B 4-bit, overridable via environment variable |
| **Citation contract** | `[chunk_id]` regex + set-membership validation; hallucinated citations explicitly surfaced |
| **Refusal contract** | Exact-phrase match: *"I cannot answer this from the provided sources."* |
| **Analyst surface** | Local Streamlit application with green citation badges and red hallucination indicators |
| **Evaluation** | N=70 hand-crafted benchmark (44 answerable + 26 refusal); structural scoring |
| **Production-default headline** | 0.598 citation_recall (all); 0.798 excluding retrieval misses; **26/26** refusals; **0** hallucinations |
| **Headline weakness** | Cross-document subset (0.233); retrieval miss rate 25% |
| **Latency** | ~23s (Gemma) / ~3–4s (Qwen) |
| **Scope** | Research artefact / proof of concept · not a production system |

---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Production Readiness
Cedant is a research artefact. It should not be used for live underwriting decisions, regulatory submissions, or any work product where citation correctness has external consequences.
:::
